title: Production managers and directors in construction
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. (Based on 15 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with supervisory personnel, owners, contractors, or design professionals to discuss and resolve work procedures or construction problems.
Plan, schedule, or coordinate construction project activities and prepare budgets to meet deadlines.
Evaluate construction methods and assess system performance or functionality at various levels.
Interpret and explain plans and contract terms to the owner's representatives, including administrative staff, workers, or clients.
Implement new plans in response to delays or emergencies and ensure compliance with environmental regulations.
Prepare contracts or negotiate revisions with architects, consultants, or subcontractors.
Investigate damage, accidents, or delays at construction sites to ensure proper procedures are followed.
Secure third-party verification to ensure responsible design activities and compliance with LEED ratings.
Direct and supervise construction workers and assess labor requirements for dispatching to sites.
Plan and coordinate installations and ensure compliance with building and safety codes or other regulations.
Prepare and submit budget estimates and progress reports, and monitor work of contractors for adherence to schedules or budgets.
Requisition supplies or materials and order necessary construction materials to complete projects.
Conduct environmental audits and develop programs to ensure compliance with environmental standards.
Train workers in construction methods, safety procedures, and company policies while providing technical assistance as needed.
Develop or implement quality control and environmental protection programs to improve project sustainability.
Review civil design, engineering, or construction documentation to ensure compliance with government or industrial codes and regulations.
Prepare applications for environmental, building, or other required permits for construction projects.
Coordinate or direct development activities to ensure that project needs and objectives are met.
Negotiate contracts for services or materials required for construction or environmental remediation projects.
Manage project costs to stay within budget limits and prepare operational budgets for construction projects.
Create project plans, including scope, goals, tasks, resources, and schedules, for construction operations.
Monitor compliance with operational, safety, or inventory control procedures during construction activities.
Prepare reports or presentations to communicate project progress and status to stakeholders and management.
Supervise subcontractors or consultants to ensure quality of work and adherence to safety regulations or project specifications.
Develop relationships and communicate with project stakeholders, including customers, site managers, and authorities.
Track and maintain records for construction operations, ensuring accuracy and compliance with standards.
